chapters.title: — Cold Open: The Number That Changes the Consent Conversation
— Headline: Hydrops as Risk Amplifier and the Consent Implication
— Pivot + Round 1: cfDNA-SGD and Imaging Integration (Araji)
— Round 2: Structural Variation Sequencing for Partial Duplications (Qin)
— Round 3: PUV Diagnosis Timing and the Severity Confound (Pecorelli)

key_claims.claim: Intrauterine transfusion for parvovirus B19 fetal anemia carries an 18% complication rate with 10% intracranial hemorrhage; survival is halved when complications occur, and hydrops amplifies risk.
Integrating cell-free DNA single-gene disorder screening with systematic prenatal imaging improves clinical interpretation for 25 dominant conditions.
Structural variation sequencing resolved partial gene duplication architecture in all tested amniotic fluid samples, enabling VUS reclassification and improved prognostication.
Second-trimester diagnosis of posterior urethral valves is associated with significantly worse mid-term renal function compared to third-trimester detection.
The choice of umbilical artery Doppler PI reference chart substantially affects predictive accuracy for placenta-mediated fetal growth restriction outcomes.
